Campanula, also known as bellflower, is a flowering plant belonging to the family Campanulaceae. The plant is known for its bell-shaped flowers and is used in traditional medicine to provide various health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Campanula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which have been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations made from Campanula include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat conditions such as anxiety, insomnia, and skin irritations.
